---
title: "Reveal.js slides — a deck that runs your code"
description: "Author a talk in Markdown; figures are computed live at render. Present in the browser, export to PDF with one flag."
image: thumbnail.svg
image-alt: "Reveal.js slides — a deck that runs your code — Blood Doctor · Quarto"
date: "2026-07-14"
categories: [Presenting]
engine: markdown
---

This is where the story began — a badly aligned PowerPoint slide, decades ago.
Today the same idea becomes a **Reveal.js** deck: authored in Markdown, with every
figure generated by code at render, and presented full-screen in any browser.

## A slide is a heading

````markdown
---
format: revealjs
---

## Why Quarto slides?
- Author in Markdown
- **Executable** code makes figures live
- Export to PDF with one flag

## A computed figure
```{python}
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
plt.plot([0,1,2],[0,1,4]); plt.show()
```
````

Each `##` starts a new slide. Incremental bullets, speaker notes, fragments,
transitions and a logo are all one line of YAML each. Press **`E`** in the deck to
export to PDF; the same source can also target **PowerPoint** (`pptx`) or
**Beamer** for LaTeX purists.

::: {.callout-tip}
## Present it now
Open the [**live Reveal.js deck**](/outputs/slides.html) — arrow keys to advance,
`F` for full-screen, `S` for speaker notes.
:::
